initial_program:
% number of rows in the input grid
nr(NR) :- NR = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.

% number of nonzero cells in each column
col_k(C,K) :- K = #count{R : input(R, C, V), V != 0}.

% for each nonzero cell give its position index among the nonzero cells of its column
order(Ri, C, I) :- input(Ri, C, V), V != 0,
                     I = #count{Rj : input(Rj, C, Vj), Vj != 0, Rj < Ri}.

% the first row where the nonzero block of a column starts
start(C,Start) :- col_k(C,K), Start = NR - K.

% mapping: the ith nonzero cell (with colour V) ends up at row = Start + I, column C
candidate(R, C, V) :-
    order(OrigR, C, I),
    start(C, Start),
    R = Start + I,
    input(OrigR, C, V), V != 0.

% a cell (R,C) is part of the output block iff there exists a candidate for it
candidate_exists(R, C) :- candidate(R, C, V).

% every input cell must be output exactly once
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).

% guess a colour for each output position
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% constraints enforcing the transformation
% nonzero cells that belong to a block may not be zero
:- output(R, C, 0), candidate_exists(R, C).

% the colour of a block cell must equal the original colour
:- output(R, C, Color), candidate(R, C, V), Color != V.

% cells that are not part of a block must stay zero
:- output(R, C, Color), Color != 0, not candidate_exists(R, C).

rounds.program_after:
% number of rows in the input grid
nr(NR) :- NR = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.

column(C) :- true.
col_k(C,K) :- column(C), K = #count{R : input(R, C, V), V != 0).

% for each nonzero cell give its position index among the nonzero cells of its column
order(Ri, C, I) :- input(Ri, C, V), V != 0,
                     I = #count{Rj : input(Rj, C, Vj), Vj != 0, Rj < Ri}.

row(NR) :- true.
start(C,Start) :- col_k(C,K), row(NR), Start = NR - K.

% mapping: the ith nonzero cell (with colour V) ends up at row = Start + I, column C
candidate(R, C, V) :-
    order(OrigR, C, I),
    start(C, Start),
    R = Start + I,
    input(OrigR, C, V), V != 0.

% a cell (R,C) is part of the output block iff there exists a candidate for it
candidate_exists(R, C) :- candidate(R, C, V).

% every input cell must be output exactly once
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).

% guess a colour for each output position
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).
color_of(R,C,Color) :- output(R, C, Color).
% constraints enforcing the transformation
% nonzero cells that belong to a block may not be zero
:- output(R, C, 0), candidate_exists(R, C).

% the colour of a block cell must equal the original colour
:- output(R, C, Color), candidate(R, C, V), Color != V.

% cells that are not part of a block must stay zero
:- output(R, C, Color), Color != 0, not candidate_exists(R, C).

rounds.program_after:
% number of rows in the input grid
nr(NR) :- NR = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.

column(C) :- true.
col_k(C,K) :- column(C), K = #count{R : input(R, C, V), V != 0).

% for each nonzero cell give its position index among the nonzero cells of its column
order(Ri, C, I) :- input(Ri, C, V), V != 0,
                     I = #count{Rj : input(Rj, C, Vj), Vj != 0, Rj < Ri}.

row(NR) :- true.
start(C,Start) :- col_k(C,K), row(NR), Start = NR - K.

% mapping: the ith nonzero cell (with colour V) ends up at row = Start + I, column C
candidate(R, C, V) :-
    order(OrigR, C, I),
    start(C, Start),
    R = Start + I,
    input(OrigR, C, V), V != 0.

% a cell (R,C) is part of the output block iff there exists a candidate for it
candidate_exists(R, C) :- candidate(R, C, V).

% every input cell must be output exactly once
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).

% guess a colour for each output position
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).
color_of(R,C,Color) :- output(R, C, Color).
% constraints enforcing the transformation
% nonzero cells that belong to a block may not be zero
:- output(R, C, 0), candidate_exists(R, C).

% the colour of a block cell must equal the original colour
:- output(R, C, Color), candidate(R, C, V), Color != V.

% cells that are not part of a block must stay zero
:- output(R, C, Color), Color != 0, not candidate_exists(R, C).
errors_after: <block>:5:63-64: error: syntax error, unexpected ), expecting } or ;

final_program:
% number of rows in the input grid
nr(NR) :- NR = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.

column(C) :- true.
col_k(C,K) :- column(C), K = #count{R : input(R, C, V), V != 0).

% for each nonzero cell give its position index among the nonzero cells of its column
order(Ri, C, I) :- input(Ri, C, V), V != 0,
                     I = #count{Rj : input(Rj, C, Vj), Vj != 0, Rj < Ri}.

row(NR) :- true.
start(C,Start) :- col_k(C,K), row(NR), Start = NR - K.

% mapping: the ith nonzero cell (with colour V) ends up at row = Start + I, column C
candidate(R, C, V) :-
    order(OrigR, C, I),
    start(C, Start),
    R = Start + I,
    input(OrigR, C, V), V != 0.

% a cell (R,C) is part of the output block iff there exists a candidate for it
candidate_exists(R, C) :- candidate(R, C, V).

% every input cell must be output exactly once
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).

% guess a colour for each output position
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).
color_of(R,C,Color) :- output(R, C, Color).
% constraints enforcing the transformation
% nonzero cells that belong to a block may not be zero
:- output(R, C, 0), candidate_exists(R, C).

% the colour of a block cell must equal the original colour
:- output(R, C, Color), candidate(R, C, V), Color != V.

% cells that are not part of a block must stay zero
:- output(R, C, Color), Color != 0, not candidate_exists(R, C).
